title: Reverse a Linked list II

content.problemStatement:
Write a program to reverse a linked list recursively.


Input Format
The first input line will contain a single integer n, which denotes the number of nodes in the linked list.

The second line will contain n space-separated integers, where each integer represents the data of a node in the linked list.



Output Format
The output should be the reversed linked list printed as a space-separated sequence of node data.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N (0 ≤ N ≤ 100), the number of nodes in the linked list. The second line contains N space‑separated integers representing the node data. If N is 0 the second line may be omitted.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line. If the list is empty print the word NULL. Otherwise print the data of the reversed linked list as a space‑separated sequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation: One test case. The list 1→2→3→4 is reversed to 4→3→2→1 and printed.
The original list -1→0→1→-10 becomes -10→1→0→-1 after reversal.

solution.approach: We build the linked list as given, then reverse it using the classic recursive method: for a node, recursively reverse the rest of the list, attach the current node at the end, and set its next to null. This runs in linear time and uses only the recursion stack. After reversal we traverse the list once to produce the output. The algorithm is optimal because each node is visited a constant number of times.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the linked list plus O(N) recursion stack (worst case)

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{static class Node{int data;Node next;Node(int d){data=d;}}static Node reverseRecursive(Node head){if(head==null||head.next==null)return head;Node rest=reverseRecursive(head.next);head.next.next=head;head.next=null;return rest;}public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder out=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());for(int t=0;t<T;t++){String line;while((line=br.readLine())!=null&&line.trim().isEmpty()){}int n=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());Node head=null,tail=null;if(n>0){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());for(int i=0;i<n;i++){int val=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());Node node=new Node(val);if(head==null)head=node;else tail.next=node;tail=node;}head=reverseRecursive(head);for(Node cur=head;cur!=null;cur=cur.next){out.append(cur.data);if(cur.next!=null)out.append(' ');}out.append('\n');}else{out.append("NULL\n");}}System.out.print(out.toString());}}
